brief_justification: Correctly identifies the employment-unchanged assumption as the critical flaw, matching ground truth; traces second/third-order effects including hours cuts, job loss, and automation with quantified examples ($20,800 at 20 hrs/week); explicitly flags why this is 'most critical rather than just one of many,' showing task-level self-awareness, though no explicit bias check; applies a counterfactual check with specific numbers but doesn't re-derive from a fully independent angle; acknowledges the assumption 'is precisely the point economists debate most' without overstating certainty; mentions alternative employer responses (hours cuts, elimination, automation) but doesn't evaluate other candidate 'most critical' assumptions the question could have targeted; every conclusion is traceable to explicit arithmetic and a clear logical chain a third party could reproduce.
